title: 10 best herbal tinctures for motion sickness
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for motion sickness are liquid extracts made from plants that help alleviate nausea, dizziness, and discomfort caused by motion. These natural remedies work by regulating digestive processes, calming the nervous system, and improving blood flow. Effective herbal tinctures for motion sickness include Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Passiflora incarnata, Avena sativa, Piper methysticum, Valeriana officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Piper nigrum, Centella asiatica, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. If left untreated, motion sickness can significantly impact daily life, causing missed work, cancelled vacations, and strained relationships due to the inability to travel or engage in activities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with motion sickness because it contains a unique combination of compounds that work synergistically to calm the nervous system. The active ingredients in kava, such as kavalactones and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are common triggers for motion sickness. By soothing the nervous system, kava tinctures can help alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, nausea, and vomiting, allowing individuals to travel with greater comfort and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Kava's anxiolytic effects help to reduce feelings of nausea and discomfort associated with motion sickness, allowing the body to better cope with the physical sensations caused by movement.

Sedative properties: Kava's sedative properties can help to slow down the body's physiological responses to motion, reducing the severity of symptoms such as dizziness, lightheadedness, and queasiness.

Muscle relaxant properties: Kava's muscle relaxant effects can help to reduce muscle spasms and cramps associated with motion sickness, promoting a sense of calm and comfort during travel or activity.
remedy_constituents: Kavalactones (Specifically, Dihydrokavain and Kavain): These compounds have been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce the nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Kavain Glycosides: These glycosides are believed to have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help to alleviate motion sickness symptoms such as dizziness and disorientation.

Stilbenoids (Specifically, Piperstilbene and Piperstilbene-3-glucoside): These compounds have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Piper methysticum contain kavalactones, which may help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Roots: Roots of the plant also contain kavalactones, which may contribute to the plant's potential benefits in treating motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a quantity of fresh or dried piper methysticum roots weighing around 30-50 grams.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 70% ethanol in a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with motion sickness because of its unique ability to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of unease and discomfort. The tincture's natural compounds have a soothing effect on the body, helping to alleviate symptoms such as dizziness and nausea that often accompany motion sickness. Additionally, maidenhair tree's anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce swelling in the inner ear, which can contribute to motion sickness. By addressing these underlying causes, maidenhair tree tinctures provide effective relief for those prone to motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms such as dizziness and nausea.

Vasodilatory effects: The tannins in Maidenhair Tree tinctures help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing the constriction that can contribute to motion sickness symptoms like lightheadedness and vertigo.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and stomach lining, which is thought to contribute to motion sickness symptoms such as nausea and dizziness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with motion sickness by stabilizing and improving blood vessel function, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness and nausea associated with motion sickness.

Bilobalide: This terpene compound may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to motion sickness, and also has a protective effect on the vestibular system, which is responsible for balance and spatial orientation.

Ginkgolides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the inner ear and protecting the vestibular system from damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are used to make tinctures for motion sickness because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate nausea and dizziness.

Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used to make tinctures for motion sickness because they contain ginkgolic acids, which have been found to have anti-emetic properties that can help prevent vomiting and other symptoms of motion sickness.

Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba are used to make tinctures for motion sickness because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, similar to the leaves, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka for the tincture.
Combine the ginkgo biloba leaves and vodka in a clean glass jar for a 2-week maceration period.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it every day to ensure even extraction.
After 2 weeks strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with motion sickness because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, which is often disrupted by the conflicting signals sent from the inner ear, eyes, and balance organs. The menthol in peppermint also has a calming effect on the stomach and can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Additionally, peppermint's cooling properties can help relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, further reducing symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint's digestive stimulating properties can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by increasing gut motility and reducing spasms.

Antispasmodic Agent: Peppermint's antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can become constricted during motion sickness, thereby reducing discomfort and improving digestion.

Natural Anti-Inflammatory: Peppermint contains natural anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to symptoms of motion sickness such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with motion sickness by stimulating the brain's trigeminal nerve, which can reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid helps with motion sickness by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Linalool: Linalool helps with motion sickness by its sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can reduce anxiety and stress that exacerbate motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol and menthone, which have natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Stems: Contain menthone and other compounds that help to calm the stomach and alleviate nausea.
Roots: Have a higher concentration of menthol, which helps to ease digestive discomfort and reduce motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers early in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2500ml of 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 1 minute.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with motion sickness because of its calming properties, which work to soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms such as dizziness and nausea. The tincture's ability to relax the body and mind can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress that often exacerbate motion sickness. Additionally, lavender's natural anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, further contributing to its effectiveness in alleviating motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-anxiolytic properties: Lavender tincture's anxiolytic effects help to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that often exacerbate motion sickness.
Gastroprotective properties: Lavender tincture's ability to soothe and protect the digestive tract can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Vomiting-suppressing properties: The terpenes present in lavender tincture have been shown to decrease the frequency and severity of vomiting, providing relief from the debilitating symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as a natural anti-emetic and anxiolytic, helping to reduce nausea and alleviate symptoms associated with motion sickness.
Linalyl acetate: As another terpene, it contributes to the calming effects of lavender, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate motion sickness.
Lavandulol: This terpene has been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and disorientation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their calming effects, which can help alleviate the anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness.
Flowers: They contain compounds with a sedative effect, which can help to reduce nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness.
Stems: They are used in combination with flowers and leaves to create a more potent tincture that can help to reduce symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn or midday when they are in full bloom.
Steep the dried flowers in 2 cups of 80 proof vodka or brandy in a dark glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place away from children and pets indefinitely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with motion sickness because of its natural ability to ease nausea and alleviate stomach discomfort. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, stimulate digestive enzymes, which can help to break down food more efficiently and reduce the feeling of queasiness. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms associated with motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Digestive anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and digestive tract, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Gastrointestinal stimulant effects: Ginger's ability to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea helps to regulate gut function, reducing feelings of queasiness and discomfort often experienced during motion sickness.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties: Ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by motion sickness, which can lead to inflammation and tissue damage, further exacerbating symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These iridoid glycosides have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, exhibit antiemetic effects by inhibiting the serotonin receptors in the brain, thereby reducing the symptoms of motion sickness.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has a sedative effect on the stomach, which helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, compounds that have anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties.
Roots: Roots are used as they contain a higher concentration of shogaol, a compound that has been shown to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Stems: Stems are used because they contain a smaller amount of gingerols, which can still provide relief from motion sickness symptoms when combined with other herbs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale roots in late summer or early fall when they are mature and dry.
Wash the roots with clean water to remove any dirt or debris then pat them dry.
Chop the washed roots into small pieces about 1-2 inches in size to increase surface area.
Mix the chopped roots with 80 proof ethanol in a ratio of 1:2 by weight and let steep for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids then discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with motion sickness because of its ability to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach. The soothing properties of chamomile help to ease nausea and vomiting, allowing passengers to feel more comfortable during travel. Additionally, chamomile's natural anti-anxiety effects can also help to alleviate stress and anxiety related to motion sickness, further reducing symptoms. By taking a chamomile tincture before or during travel, individuals can find relief from the discomforts of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and soothing effects: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness, while its soothing effects calm the stomach and digestive system.
Antispasmodic activity: Chamomile's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the stomach and digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms.

Calming and sedative properties: Chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can help to reduce anxiety and stress related to motion sickness, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing feelings of queasiness.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that has a sedative effect on the body, helping to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Chamazulene: An isobutylbenzene derivative that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate the nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high apigenin content, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Leaves: They contain similar compounds to the flowers and are sometimes used as a substitute or in combination with flowers to enhance the tincture's effectiveness.
Seeds: The seeds also contain apigenin and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, although their use may be less common than flowers and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak bloom when the flowers are fully open and dry.
Chop 1 part of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and mix with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it once a day for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Transfer the tincture to smaller glass bottles with dropper lids and label them clearly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with motion sickness because its active compounds, including valerenic acids and isovaleric acid, have a calming effect on the nervous system. This can help to reduce the body's response to the conflicting sensory information caused by motion, such as the visual and vestibular inputs that trigger nausea and dizziness. By soothing the nervous system, valerian tinctures can alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, making travel smoother and more comfortable for those prone to this condition.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing the anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian's ability to relax muscle spasms can help alleviate the physical discomfort and nausea associated with motion sickness.

Vascular relaxation: Valerian's vasodilatory effects can help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the body, which may help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric Acid: Helps with motion sickness by acting as a natural sedative and anxiolytic, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate symptoms.
Valerenic Acid: Contributes to motion sickness relief by modulating the GABA receptor, which can help to calm the nervous system and alleviate nausea and dizziness.

Valeranone: Plays a role in motion sickness relief by acting as a sedative and anxiolytic, similar to isovaleric acid, and also by exerting a relaxing effect on the smooth muscle in the stomach, which can help to reduce nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are commonly used in tinctures for motion sickness due to their high valerenic acid content, which has sedative properties.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes are also used in tinctures for motion sickness due to their ability to help regulate the nervous system and alleviate nausea.
Seeds: Valerian seeds are sometimes used in tinctures for motion sickness due to their potential to aid in digestion and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in the fall when the plant is in its dormant stage for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours to reduce moisture.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and use a ratio of one part plant material to five parts solvent such as 80 percent ethanol for tincture production.
Steep the plant material in the solvent for four to six weeks in a glass container with a lid to allow for extraction.
Filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with motion sickness because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides that work together to stabilize the inner ear and reduce symptoms of nausea and vertigo. The maypop plant's bioactive compounds have been shown to inhibit the release of serotonin, a neurotransmitter associated with feelings of queasiness and dizziness, thereby alleviating the discomfort and distress caused by motion sickness. As a result, herbal maypop tinctures can provide natural relief from the debilitating effects of motion sickness, allowing individuals to enjoy activities without worrying about feeling unwell.
remedy_properties: Sedative and relaxing properties: Maypop's sedative effects can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and nausea associated with motion sickness.
Antispasmodic properties: Maypop's antispasmodic compounds can help relieve muscle cramps and spasms that often occur in response to motion sickness.
Gastroprotective properties: Maypop's ability to soothe the digestive tract can help reduce nausea, vomiting, and stomach discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: Harmane, a beta-carboline alkaloid, helps alleviate motion sickness by exerting anxiolytic effects and reducing anxiety-related symptoms associated with nausea and vomiting.

Flavonoids (specifically, vitexin and isovitexin): These flavonoids contribute to the antivertigo and antiemetic effects of Passiflora incarnata by modulating neurotransmitter activity and reducing inflammation in the central nervous system.

Rotundine: Rotundine, another beta-carboline alkaloid, helps alleviate motion sickness by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which promotes relaxation and reduces nausea-related symptoms by inhibiting the central nervous system's response to motion stimuli.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of flavonoids and other compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate motion sickness.
Fruits: The ripe fruits of Passiflora incarnata are rich in vitamins and minerals, and their extract is believed to help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Roots: The roots of the plant are said to contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, which can help to calm the digestive system and alleviate motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in the morning.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for solvent.
Combine the chopped plant material with 4 parts of 80% ethanol or glycerin in a clean.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container to.
Bottle the final tincture in dark glass containers and label it clearly with the date and.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with motion sickness because it contains rosmarinic acid, a natural compound that has a calming effect on the nervous system. This property helps to reduce feelings of anxiety and unease often associated with motion sickness. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea. By promoting relaxation and alleviating symptoms, herbal lemon balm tinctures can provide natural relief from the discomfort of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Calming effect on the digestive system: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties can help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing nausea and stomach upset associated with motion sickness.

Antiemetic properties: The essential oils present in lemon balm tinctures, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to have a direct antiemetic effect, helping to prevent vomiting and reduce symptoms of motion sickness.

Anxiolytic effects: Lemon balm's ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety can help alleviate the mental and emotional stress that exacerbates motion sickness, making it easier for individuals to cope with symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps alleviate motion sickness by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its anti-emetic properties.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, potentially reducing the severity of motion sickness by inhibiting the vomiting reflex.

β-Nocotrienol: A vitamin E derivative that has been reported to have anti-emetic properties, possibly by reducing the release of neurotransmitters that stimulate nausea and vomiting during motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their calming and anti-nausea properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain a higher concentration of melissic acid and other compounds that help to alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
Stems: The stems are used for their ability to retain the medicinal properties of the plant and can be used in tinctures as a more readily available alternative to the leaves or flowers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried.
Clean the plant material by gently removing dirt and debris using a soft brush.
Combine the cleaned melissa plant material with a solvent such as 80 proof vodka.
Allow the initial tincture mixture to steep in a glass container for four to six weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with motion sickness because of its natural calming properties, which help to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. The herb's ability to relax the muscles and calm the nerves also helps to alleviate the symptoms of dizziness and disorientation that often accompany motion sickness. Additionally, lemon verbena has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the inner ear, a common cause of motion sickness. Overall, using herbal lemon verbena tinctures can provide relief from motion sickness and make travel or other activities less unpleasant.
remedy_properties: Calming and anti-anxiety effects: Lemon verbena's calming properties can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness, making it easier for individuals to cope with symptoms.
Digestive soothing: The tincture's ability to soothe digestive issues such as nausea, cramping, and bloating can help alleviate the uncomfortable physical sensations associated with motion sickness.

Antispasmodic properties: Lemon verbena's antispasmodic effects can help to relax muscles and reduce spasms in the stomach, which are common symptoms of motion sickness, thereby providing relief from discomfort and distress.
remedy_constituents: Verbenalin: This alkaloid has been shown to have anti-emetic properties, which help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Isorhapontigenin: This phenolic compound has been found to have a protective effect on the inner ear and balance system, which can be disrupted during motion sickness, reducing the severity of symptoms.

Rhaponticin: This iridoid glycoside has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of essential oils, which have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain compounds that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help with motion sickness.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their calming effect and high concentration of essential oils, which can help to reduce nausea and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis flowers and leaves in the morning when the dew is still present on the plants.
Clean the freshly harvested verbena officinalis flowers and leaves thoroughly with a soft brush to remove any dirt.
Combine 1 part of fresh verbena officinalis flowers and leaves with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow the tincture to steep.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tinctures helps with motion sickness because of its natural ability to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of anxiety and discomfort. The herb's flavonoids and caffeic acid work together to relax the muscles, ease tension, and soothe the stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms such as dizziness, nausea, and vomiting. By promoting a sense of balance and equilibrium, skullcap tinctures can help individuals tolerate motion more comfortably, making it an effective natural remedy for those prone to motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Skullcap's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by motion sickness, which can exacerbate symptoms like nausea and dizziness.

GABA-ergic activity: The tincture's active compounds, such as baicalin and scutellarin, bind to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, a common precursor to motion sickness.

Stomach-soothing properties: Skullcap has been traditionally used to calm digestive upset and ease nausea, which is often a primary symptom of motion sickness, helping to alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a higher concentration of the active compounds, such as scutellarein and baicalin, which have been shown to have anti-nausea properties.
Barks: The barks are used due to their rich content of flavonoids, which help to stabilize blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality scutellaria lateriflora flowers, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pests.
Cleanly chop the harvested flowers into small pieces to facilitate extraction.
Combine 250 grams of chopped flowers with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to promote extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dill tinctures helps with motion sickness because of its natural ability to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. The herb contains compounds that have a direct effect on the digestive system, soothing irritation and inflammation caused by motion-induced discomfort. Additionally, dill's antispasmodic properties help relax the muscles in the abdomen, further reducing feelings of queasiness and discomfort. As a result, herbal dill tinctures provide a safe and natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Anticholinergic effect: Dill's anticholinergic properties help to relax the stomach muscles, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Carminative action: The volatile oils in dill tincture stimulate digestion, reduce bloating, and relieve pressure on the stomach, helping to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Dill's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to soothe the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dill plant contain compounds that have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Seeds: Dill seeds are rich in carvone, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as queasiness and stomach upset.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50 grams of dried anethum graveolens leaves and 70% proof vodka for the tincture preparation.
Combine the dried herb material with 1000 milliliters of the vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring adequate coverage.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily for two weeks to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding solids and reserving the resulting tincture.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at 2-8 degrees celsius, allowing it to mature for four weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew tinctures helps with motion sickness because its active compound, parthenolide, has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When taken sublingually or mixed with water, feverfew's bioactive compounds are quickly absorbed into the bloodstream, targeting the digestive system and reducing inflammation caused by motion-induced nausea and discomfort. As a natural remedy, feverfew tinctures work synergistically with the body to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, making it an effective supplement for travelers and individuals prone to motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide content: Feverfew tincture contains parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in feverfew tincture, such as apigenin and luteolin, have vasodilatory effects, which can help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of motion sickness.

Antioxidant activity: Feverfew tincture has been shown to possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress caused by prolonged exposure to motion stimuli, such as car travel or boat rides.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Flowers: Contain a high concentration of parthenolide, a bioactive compound that has been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried tanacetum parthenium flowers in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Combine one part dried tanacetum parhenium flowers with four parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking daily for 15 minutes.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Label and date the final product, using a dropper to extract 20-40 drops as needed before travel or during symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with motion sickness because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. When taken before traveling by air, land, or sea, the tincture can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea, dizziness, and lightheadedness. The tincture's calming effects on the digestive system also help reduce stomach upset and discomfort associated with motion sickness, allowing individuals to enjoy their travels without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, which helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice tinctures contain flavonoids, such as liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help to reduce the severity of motion sickness symptoms.

Triterpenoid saponins: Licorice root is rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have been found to have antihistamine-like properties, helping to relax smooth muscle and alleviate stomach cramps and discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used in medicinal tinctures as they contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to make medicinal tinctures for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried licorice root (glycyrrhiza glabra) and chop it into small pieces for processing.
Combine the chopped licorice root with 1000 milliliters of vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking it daily to ensure uniform extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid tincture.
Transfer the tincture to smaller dark glass bottles, labeling each with the date and contents, and store in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese thoroughwort tinctures helps with motion sickness because it contains a unique combination of bioactive compounds that target the underlying physiological processes contributing to this condition. The herb's volatile oils and flavonoids have been shown to effectively calm the stomach, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate feelings of dizziness and disorientation commonly associated with motion sickness. By addressing these symptoms at their root cause, Chinese thoroughwort tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for alleviating the discomfort and distress caused by motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Zhi Mu's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the digestive tract, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms such as nausea and dizziness.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic property of Zhi Mu tincture helps to relax muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms like vomiting and abdominal pain.

Calming and sedative property: Zhi Mu's calming and sedative properties help to soothe the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress related to motion sickness, which can further alleviate symptoms like nausea and dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to stabilize the digestive system and alleviate nausea associated with motion sickness.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems, contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to calm the stomach during travel.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality bupleurum falcatum roots weighing about 50 grams, ensure freshness and dryness.
Chop the harvested bupleurum falcatum roots into small pieces weighing around 20 grams each piece.
Combine the chopped bupleurum falcatum roots with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling it to the top leaving no air gaps.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place allowing the mixture to steep for 2 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding solids after filtering resulting tincture with 1:3 ratio of herbs to solvent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Betony tinctures helps with motion sickness because it targets the gut, where the problem originates. The plant contains a unique compound called beta-amyrin, which has been shown to stimulate digestion and calm inflammation in the digestive tract. When taken before or during travel, betony tincture can help regulate stomach acid production, reduce nausea, and alleviate vomiting caused by motion sickness. remedy_properties: Calming effects on the nervous system: Betony's calming properties help to reduce feelings of anxiety and tension, which can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms.
Antispasmodic properties: Betony's antispasmodic properties help to relax the stomach muscles and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Betony's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate digestive discomfort, which can be a common symptom of motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for making medicinal tinctures for motion sickness because they contain a high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have anti-nausea properties.

Barks: The barks are used to make tinctures for motion sickness because they possess a unique combination of alkaloids and phenolic compounds that help to calm the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stachys officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Wash the plant material gently with clean water to remove dirt and debris carefully.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a clean glass jar filled.
Mix 50% vodka or other high-proof alcohol with 50% distilled water and pour it over the packed plant material slowly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks shaking occasionally before straining and filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with motion sickness because of its ability to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea. The antispasmodic properties of yarrow help to relax the stomach muscles, preventing them from contracting in response to turbulent movement. Additionally, yarrow's anti-inflammatory effects can soothe the inner ear, where balance and equilibrium are regulated. By addressing these physiological mechanisms, herbal yarrow tinctures provide natural relief from motion sickness symptoms, allowing individuals to travel and engage in activities without discomfort or distress.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of motion sickness like nausea and stomach cramps.

Vasodilatory properties: The vasodilating effects of yarrow tincture's alkaloids, including achilleine and santonin, help to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow to the brain, which can reduce dizziness and lightheadedness associated with motion sickness.

Antispasmodic properties: Yarrow tincture's antispasmodic compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help relax smooth muscle contractions in the digestive tract and stomach, reducing symptoms of nausea, vomiting, and abdominal cramps commonly experienced during motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which help calm digestive issues and reduce nausea associated with motion sickness.
Flowers: Contain apiol, a compound that helps to regulate blood flow and alleviate symptoms of dizziness and lightheadedness caused by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and stems in the early morning or evening when essential oils are most concentrated.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material by gently shaking off any debris and air-drying it for 24 hours.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion and combine with a 40% grain-based vodka at a ratio of 1:4.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking daily, allowing the plant's active compounds to dissolve into the liquid.

Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and discard the solids, then transfer the resulting herbal remedy to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, storing them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree tinctures helps with motion sickness because they contain a unique compound called agnuside, which has natural anti-nausea properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vitex agnus-castus fruits in mid-summer and dry them thoroughly to remove excess moisture.
Chop the dried fruit into small pieces and combine with vodka at a ratio of 1:3 by weight in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow for thorough extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Transfer the final tincture to amber glass bottles, label with date and contents, and store in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality artemisia vulgaris leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the freshly harvested material into small pieces and combine with 50% vodka by weight in a glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica tinctures helps with motion sickness because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach and digestive tract. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica root and leaf, ensure quality and potency by inspecting for signs of disease or pest infestation.
Chop the harvested angelica archangelica into small pieces, allowing for maximum exposure to solvent in subsequent steps.
Combine 250 grams of chopped angelica archangelica with 1 liter of high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, ensure jar is filled to the top to minimize oxidation.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking jar daily to facilitate extraction and infusion process.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard solids and label the tincture with date and ingredients used.
